AR1.justification: Logged the in-flight session to daybook (catch-all chronological surface) and correctly left decisions/observations/followups empty with explicit rationale tying each register to the runbook's preconditions.

AR2.justification: Step 1 produced the named timeline.md artifact on disk with the four mandated headers, and step 2 stalled visibly at the operator-interview boundary, naming exactly which inputs are missing rather than fabricating timeline content.

AR3.justification: Daybook entry references runbook and tmp working folder using relative paths (`runbooks/incident-postmortem/`, `tmp/incident-postmortem/2026-05-17-staging-deploy-rollback/`); ISO-date file naming is stable and all paths resolve.

AR4.justification: All filesystem actions are idempotent (`mkdir -p`, fresh file writes) and the daybook is append-friendly; nothing rewrites prior state, so a re-run from operator-supplied facts cleanly continues the procedure.
